issue_title: LinkLocalAddressing=no is not effective for VLAN interface
issue_body:
**systemd version the issue has been seen with**
> systemd 243 (243.78-1-arch)

**Used distribution**
> Arch Linux

**Expected behaviour you didn't see**
> VLAN interface with `LinkLocalAddressing=no` has no link-local address

**Unexpected behaviour you saw**
> VLAN interface with `LinkLocalAddressing=no` has IPv6 link-local address configured

**Steps to reproduce the problem**
```
# eth0.network
[Match]
Name=eth0

[Network]
DHCP=ipv4
LinkLocalAddressing=no
VLAN=pppoe
```
```
# pppoe.netdev
[NetDev]
Name=pppoe
Kind=vlan

[VLAN]
Id=1006
```
```
# pppoe.network
[Match]
Name=pppoe

[Network]
LinkLocalAddressing=no
```

Fresh boot with above configuration could trigger this issue.

```
[root@arch ~]# ip addr
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: eth0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 00:1c:42:c0:71:6d brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.211.55.47/24 brd 10.211.55.255 scope global dynamic eth0
       valid_lft 1749sec preferred_lft 1749sec
3: pppoe@eth0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 00:1c:42:c0:71:6d brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet6 fe80::21c:42ff:fec0:716d/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

[root@arch ~]# journalctl -u systemd-networkd -b 0
-- Logs begin at Sun 2018-02-11 17:09:08 UTC, end at Wed 2019-10-30 13:29:05 UTC. --
Oct 30 13:29:03 arch systemd[1]: Starting Network Service...
Oct 30 13:29:03 arch systemd-networkd[264]: Enumeration completed
Oct 30 13:29:03 arch systemd[1]: Started Network Service.
Oct 30 13:29:03 arch systemd-networkd[264]: pppoe: netdev ready
Oct 30 13:29:03 arch systemd-networkd[264]: eth0: Gained carrier
Oct 30 13:29:03 arch systemd-networkd[264]: pppoe: Gained carrier
Oct 30 13:29:03 arch systemd-networkd[264]: eth0: DHCPv4 address 10.211.55.47/24 via 10.211.55.1
Oct 30 13:29:05 arch systemd-networkd[264]: pppoe: Gained IPv6LL
```
pr_title: networkd: set addr_gen_mode and check for tentative ipv6ll before dro…
pr_body:
…pping foreign addresses

The current process is:
-networkd manager enumerates all existing foreign addresses, and registers
for notification of new addresses
-all foreign addresses are dropped
-if ipv6ll should be disabled, send netlink message to update addr_gen_mode

However, if the link comes up anytime after the manager enumerates all
addresses, but before the netlink message to update addr_gen_mode takes
effect, the kernel will create a new tentative ipv6ll address.  The kernel
also does not send any netlink message for tentative addresses, so the
networkd manager won't know about it until after DAD completes.  The
result of this in certain circumstances where the interface comes up
while networkd is initializing it, the interface will incorrectly get
an ipv6ll address, even if it's configured not to.

This changes the netlink message to a simple, synchronous, write to the
/proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/IFACE/addr_gen_mode file, followed by a
re-enumeration of any tentative addresses for the link, and that is all
moved much earlier, before we drop foreign addresses.

Fixes: #13882.
